chapters.title: Matter and Its States
key_facts: Matter has mass and occupies space
Solids have definite shape and volume
Gases have no fixed shape or volume
Sublimation: solid directly to gas (e.g., dry ice, camphor)
misconceptions: Ice and glass are both solids, so they must behave the same — FALSE
chapters.hero_hook: Why does a camphor ball disappear without melting?

chapters.title: Elements, Compounds and Mixtures
key_facts: Compound: fixed ratio of elements, new properties
Mixture: no fixed ratio, properties of constituents retained
chapters.hero_hook: How do forensic scientists separate ink at a crime scene?

chapters.title: Atomic Structure
key_facts: Atom is smallest unit of matter
Carbon-14 used in dating ancient artefacts
equations: Atomic number Z = number of protons
chapters.hero_hook: If atoms are mostly empty space, why can't you walk through walls?

chapters.title: Chemical Reactions
chapters.hero_hook: Why does bread rise and turn brown in an oven?

chapters.title: Acids, Bases and Salts
key_facts: pH < 7 = acidic, pH 7 = neutral, pH > 7 = basic
Litmus: red in acid, blue in base
chapters.hero_hook: Your stomach is more acidic than lemon juice — and it doesn't dissolve itself. Why?

chapters.title: The Periodic Table
key_facts: Modern Periodic Law: properties are periodic functions of atomic number
chapters.hero_hook: How Mendeleev predicted the existence of elements not yet discovered

chapters.title: Chemical Bonding
topics: Properties of Ionic vs Covalent Compounds
key_facts: NaCl is ionic; H₂O is covalent
Ionic compounds have high melting points
Covalent compounds are often gases or liquids at room temperature
chapters.hero_hook: Why is salt a solid and methane a gas at room temperature?

chapters.title: Mole Concept
key_facts: Stoichiometry: mole ratios from balanced equations
chapters.hero_hook: How many atoms are in a single grain of sand?

chapters.title: The Language of Chemistry
chapters.hero_hook: The secret grammar rules that chemists use to write the language of matter

chapters.title: Electrolysis
key_facts: Faraday's First Law: mass deposited ∝ charge passed
chapters.hero_hook: How a simple salt-water experiment can split water into the gases that fuel rockets

chapters.title: Organic Chemistry
chapters.hero_hook: Carbon: the atom responsible for all life, plastics, diamonds, and graphene

chapters.title: Metallurgy
key_facts: Metals high in reactivity series extracted by electrolysis
Iron extracted by blast furnace reduction
chapters.hero_hook: The ancient alchemy that turned rock into the tools that built civilisation
